headlineSummary: OpenAI drew the most negative reaction, while Anthropic was a bit more favorable and Google Gemini was mixed-neutral on limited evidence [E1][E2][E3].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was discussed negatively overall, with criticism of Codex output and OpenAI’s business strategy outweighing one positive coding anecdote [E1][E2][E3].
Google Gemini had a mixed-neutral reading on limited evidence, driven by one criticism of Google’s hosted-model strategy [E2].
Anthropic leaned slightly positive, with praise for Claude Code and a one-page HTML result offset by criticism tied to the same business-model discussion [E1][E2][E3].

evidence.excerpt: This website seems to have been generated by Codex - I asked Codex to create an HTML overview of a feature for my team and it made an overly produced monstrosity - complete with the same large stat boxes that were for the most part devoid of meaningful information - using the same font, colors, layout, hero section, etc. It was also terrible on mobile just like this is. In the end I had Claude produce a one-page html file that was 95% of the way there and it took minor editing to clearly explain the intent of the feature.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly names Codex and strongly criticizes its output as an 'overly produced monstrosity' that was 'terrible on mobile'.
The commenter explicitly names Claude and says it produced a one-page HTML file '95% of the way there' that needed only minor edits.

evidence.excerpt: Obviously OpenAI betrayed their stated mandate by going to the largely closed-source API-access business model, which is the one that Anthropic, Google, and xAI also adopted, i.e. the high-margin hosted model businesses, while DeepSeek, Alibaba/Qwen, Baidu/ERNIE, and Tencent/Hunyuan are breaking that model by releasing various open weight models.

annotations.rationale: The commenter explicitly says OpenAI 'betrayed their stated mandate' by adopting a closed, high-margin API business model.
Anthropic is explicitly grouped with firms criticized for adopting the same 'closed-source API-access business model' and 'high-margin hosted model businesses'.
In this AI-model-provider context, 'Google' is being criticized for adopting the same closed, high-margin hosted model strategy, which maps to Google's tracked AI offering.

evidence.excerpt: When using Codex/Claude Code with Go code I cannot count the times the agent does some change, runs a build to check for errors, find some and fix them.

annotations.rationale: The commenter credits Codex with repeatedly making code changes, building, finding errors, and fixing them.
The commenter credits Claude Code with repeatedly making code changes, building, finding errors, and fixing them.
